name: Cruachan
description: Cruachan, also known as Rathcroghan, is a significant archaeological complex in County Roscommon, Ireland, and a crucial site in Irish mythology. It was considered one of the royal sites of Connacht and a gateway to the Otherworld. Cruachan served as the seat of the Kings and Queens of Connacht, holding great political and spiritual importance. The complex consists of various monuments, including burial mounds, ringforts, and standing stones, indicating its long history and diverse uses. Mythologically, Cruachan is associated with Samhain, the Celtic festival marking the transition between summer and winter, where the boundary between worlds thinned, allowing supernatural beings to cross over. It is said that creatures and events from the Otherworld manifested at Cruachan during Samhain, including the Morrígan, a goddess of war and fate. The cave of Cruachan, known as 'Uaimh na gCat' (Cave of the Cats), is particularly significant as it was believed to be an entrance to the Otherworld, from which dangerous creatures and plagues emerged. Stories of Cruachan are interwoven with tales of heroes, gods, and supernatural events, making it a central location in Irish folklore and a testament to the rich cultural heritage of the region. Cruachan also features prominently in the Táin Bó Cúailnge (Cattle Raid of Cooley), one of the most important stories in Irish mythology, further solidifying its place in the collective imagination.
subtitle: Gateway to the Otherworld in Connacht
features: Uaimh na gCat (Cave of the Cats)

keyMyths.title: Emergence of Nemed's People
keyMyths.description: It is said that the people of Nemed emerged from the cave of Cruachan, showcasing its role as a point of origin and connection to ancient ancestors. This highlights the cave's significance as a portal and a source of life or re-emergence.

keyMyths.title: The Cattle Raid of Cooley
keyMyths.description: Cruachan is mentioned as the home of Queen Medb, a central figure in the Táin Bó Cúailnge, highlighting its political and strategic importance in this epic tale. Medb's presence at Cruachan solidifies its status as a powerful and influential royal seat.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: A comprehensive dictionary of Celtic mythological figures and places.
sources.text: A comprehensive dictionary of Celtic mythological figures and places.
